pi_items.source: Task description role 'Migration Lead' maps to O*NET SOC 11-3021 'Computer and Information Systems Manager' (plans and coordinates IT activities including infrastructure migrations).
Task description: 'platform cutover from on-premise infrastructure to cloud'. The coordination workflow (war room, runbook, go/no-go gates, rollback triggers) is standard DevOps/SRE practice.

setup_task.sh: all channel names prefixed 'atlas-' (atlas-database, atlas-networking, atlas-backend, atlas-frontend, atlas-qa). Seeded message in #engineering-announcements: 'Project Atlas: Cloud Migration Timeline Update'.
setup_task.sh line 228: seeded message states 'maintenance window 2026-03-18 01:00-05:00 UTC'. Task description matches. Verifier C2 checks topic for '2026-03-18' or '01:00'.
setup_task.sh: channels.create API calls on lines 105 (atlas-database), 122 (atlas-networking), 142 (atlas-backend), 159 (atlas-frontend), 176 (atlas-qa). All 5 confirmed created during live testing.
setup_task.sh line 193: channels.create with name 'ops-alerts'. Two alert messages seeded with IDs saved to baseline for thread verification. Confirmed during live testing.

setup_task.sh line 219: channels.create with name 'engineering-announcements'. Two historical messages seeded (vp.engineering timeline update, product.owner comms plan). Confirmed during live testing.

Task description: 'Create a private channel atlas-cutover-war-room'. setup_task.sh lines 97-100: cleanup deletes pre-existing 'atlas-cutover-war-room'. export_result.sh line 63: looks up groups.info?roomName=atlas-cutover-war-room. Verifier checks existence, privacy, topic, members.
setup_task.sh lines 83-87: create_user_if_not_exists calls for all 5 team leads. All confirmed created during live testing via users.list API.
setup_task.sh lines 88-89: create_user_if_not_exists for both. Task description: 'plus sre.oncall and cloud.architect'. Verifier C3 line 152: required_members includes both.

setup_task.sh lines 88-92: all 5 users created. vp.engineering and product.owner post seeded messages in #engineering-announcements. security.lead posts in #atlas-networking. export_result.sh checks DMs to vp.engineering, sre.oncall, db.lead.

setup_task.sh seeded messages: (1) #atlas-database db.lead: 'Pre-cutover status: GO' (line 114). (2) #atlas-networking network.lead: 'Pre-cutover status: CONDITIONAL' (line 131), final message: 'Status remains: CONDITIONAL pending production gateway restart' (line 136). (3) #atlas-backend backend.lead: 'Pre-cutover status: GO' (line 151). (4) #atlas-frontend frontend.lead: 'Pre-cutover status: GO' (line 168). (5) #atlas-qa qa.lead: validation plan finalized with 3 phases, no explicit status keyword but operationally READY (line 185). Not in task.json metadata (removed to prevent answer leak).

setup_task.sh: only #atlas-networking has status CONDITIONAL. The final message (line 136) says 'Status remains: CONDITIONAL pending production gateway restart confirmation at cutover time.' The networking lead is network.lead (Tomas Herrera). Not in task.json metadata (removed to prevent answer leak).

pi_items.verified_value: Original blocker was TLS cert expiry (2026-03-19). Cert issue resolved via CERT-2026-0312 expedited approval (new expiry 2027-03-18). NEW issue discovered: gateway TLS termination configured for TLS 1.2 only, needs TLS 1.3. Patch applied to staging, production requires 2-minute gateway restart during maintenance window. Final status: CONDITIONAL pending production gateway restart.
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh #atlas-networking messages: msg1 (CONDITIONAL, cert expiry), msg2 (cert renewal needs compliance), msg3 (expedited review request), msg4 (escalation), msg5 (UPDATE: cert resolved, TLS 1.2/1.3 issue found), msg6 (staging patched, production needs restart, status remains CONDITIONAL).

pi_items.verified_value: Payment Processing Service (PPS) has hardcoded on-prem database connection string. Must be updated AFTER database migration completes, BEFORE enabling cloud routing for PPS. Correct runbook order: DB migration -> PPS config update -> backend cloud routing.
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh #atlas-backend message (line 152): 'DB migration must complete, then PPS config update, then backend cloud routing.' Confirmed by qa.lead in same channel: 'PPS config dependency documented, Test ID: INT-PPS-CLOUD-001'.

pi_items.verified_value: WARN severity. Cloud load balancer atlas-lb-prod-01 showing intermittent 502 responses (3 in last hour). Source: synthetic monitoring probe. Not blocking.
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 207: seeded message text '[sre.oncall]: [ALERT] Pre-cutover health check WARN: Cloud load balancer atlas-lb-prod-01...'

pi_items.verified_value: CRITICAL severity. On-prem backup job for payment-db-prod failed at 22:15 UTC. Error: Disk quota exceeded on backup volume /mnt/backup-san-07. Last successful backup: 2026-03-17 18:00 UTC. This backup is a prerequisite for cutover rollback safety.
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 212: seeded message text '[sre.oncall]: [ALERT] Pre-cutover health check CRITICAL: On-prem backup job...'

pi_items.verified_value: Step 0: Gateway TLS 1.3 restart (from networking). Step 1: Database migration (847GB MongoDB replica set). Step 2: PPS config update (connection string). Step 3: QA Phase 1 validation. Step 4: Backend cloud routing (feature flag flip). Step 5: QA Phase 2 validation. Step 6: Frontend cutover with maintenance banner + DNS switch. Step 7: QA Phase 3 E2E tests.
pi_items.source: Synthesized from cross-channel constraints: networking requires gateway restart before migration starts; backend requires DB migration before PPS config before cloud routing; frontend needs maintenance banner before DNS switch; QA has 3 phases interleaved (post-db, post-backend, post-frontend).

pi_items.verified_value: Error rate > 1%, p99 latency > 800ms, database connection failures > 5/min (from SRE monitoring config). QA rollback criteria: Phase 1 failure = immediate rollback, Phase 2 failure = rollback if not resolved in 15 minutes, Phase 3 = case-by-case.
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh #atlas-qa messages: sre.oncall line 187 sets alert thresholds; qa.lead line 186 defines rollback criteria per phase.

pi_items.verified_value: All seeded messages use format '[username]: message text' via post_as() helper. Messages are posted by admin user with text prefix identifying the simulated sender. The verifier filters seeded messages in #engineering-announcements and #ops-alerts using prefix matching ([vp.engineering]:, [product.owner]:, [sre.oncall]: [ALERT]).
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh post_as() function (line 62-68): formats as '[${user}]: ${text}'. verifier.py SEEDED_MSG_PREFIXES (lines 38-41) and _is_seeded() (lines 44-46) filter these out.

privileged_info_summary: This is a fully synthetic Rocket.Chat coordination task. The environment has 7 channels seeded with 22 messages and 10 users. Team readiness statuses: Database=GO (847GB MongoDB migration ready, connection pool bump CR-4421 handled), Networking=CONDITIONAL (original cert expiry issue RESOLVED via CERT-2026-0312 expedited approval, but NEW issue: gateway TLS termination is TLS 1.2 only, needs TLS 1.3 patch requiring 2-minute production gateway restart during maintenance window), Backend=GO (14 microservices pass, but PPS has hardcoded on-prem DB connection string requiring sequenced update: DB migration first, then PPS config, then backend cloud routing), Frontend=GO (CDN/WebSocket ready, needs admin to enable maintenance-banner-atlas feature flag at cutover time), QA=READY (3-phase validation plan: Phase 1 post-DB, Phase 2 post-backend, Phase 3 post-frontend, 45 min total). Two ops alerts: (1) WARN - LB atlas-lb-prod-01 intermittent 502s, 3/hour, not blocking; (2) CRITICAL - backup job for payment-db-prod failed, disk quota exceeded on /mnt/backup-san-07, last good backup 2026-03-17 18:00 UTC, blocks rollback safety. The correct conditional team lead to DM is network.lead (Tomas Herrera). The correct runbook order is: gateway TLS 1.3 restart -> DB migration -> PPS config update -> QA Phase 1 -> backend cloud routing -> QA Phase 2 -> frontend cutover with maintenance banner + DNS switch -> QA Phase 3. Rollback triggers: error rate >1%, p99 latency >800ms, DB connection failures >5/min; QA Phase 1 fail=immediate rollback, Phase 2 fail=15min window, Phase 3=case-by-case. The war room must be PRIVATE, named 'atlas-cutover-war-room', with topic containing 'Project Atlas', 'Cutover', and '2026-03-18 01:00-05:00 UTC'. Required war room members: db.lead, network.lead, backend.lead, frontend.lead, qa.lead, sre.oncall, cloud.architect (7 total). All seeded messages use '[username]: text' format and are posted by the admin account.
